name: semiconductor-trade
topic: US-China-EU trade tensions over semiconductor exports

rubrics: Facts should cover the three major blocs and keep their positions distinct.
Facts should name specific export controls, supply-chain dependencies, and retaliatory measures.
Facts should include dated developments when the sequence changes the strategic picture.
Facts should avoid collapsing the topic into generic great-power rivalry or general tech policy.
3-6 named decision-makers: US government, China government, EU/European Commission, ASML, key semiconductor companies — not generic 'the West'.
Each player must have has-objective relationship. Objectives must reflect their distinct strategic incentives.
conflicts-with relationships must exist between competing objectives (e.g., US tech leadership vs China self-reliance).
Player entities must include playerType and knowledge array reflecting information asymmetry.
Should identify 2-3 interacting games: technology competition, trade/export controls, diplomatic alliance.
Strategies must be specific to semiconductor trade: export controls, subsidies, retaliation, coalition-building.
Each game must have timing specified.
In a full pipeline run, plays-in and has-strategy relationships should link players to games and strategies.
Must include interaction-history for US-China and US-EU semiconductor interactions.
Must reference the escalation sequence: CHIPS Act → export controls → gallium/germanium retaliation.
Trust assessments should cover US-EU alliance cohesion and US-China competitive trust.
Signaling effects should capture how US controls signal to EU and third parties.
Must include cross-game-effect or cross-game-constraint-table showing how games interact.
Equilibrium analysis should account for the 3-player dynamic, not just US-China bilateral.
Payoff matrices must have rationale and dependency references on every payoff estimate.
Signal classification should assess whether US/China public statements are cheap talk or costly signals.
Must include assumption about China's technological catch-up timeline (capability category).
Must include assumption about EU coalition stability (institutional category).
Must include assumption about information availability on each bloc's capabilities (information category).
Each assumption must have sensitivity rating and dependencies referencing prior-phase entities.
Must include escalation scenario (further controls/retaliation) and de-escalation scenario.
Must include strategic decoupling scenario as tail-risk subtype.
Scenario probabilities must sum to 95-105%.
Should distinguish between short-term (1-2 year) and structural (5-10 year) outcomes.
Central thesis must be falsifiable and reference the multi-player dynamic.
